body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` **Service directory:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` **Triggered by:** commit cdd7d8fa9537f21385a64057d03b4cc6ee71752d (post-release version bump for PR #58257) by @azure-sdk ## What Changed Initial public release (1.0.0-beta.1) of `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph`, which enables declarative provisioning of Azure Resource Graph shared queries using the Azure Provisioning library pattern. The package exposes the `ResourceGraphQuery` resource type backed by `Microsoft.ResourceGraph/queries@2024-04-01`. ## Gaps Found - **Empty "Authenticate the Client" section**: The `### Authenticate the Client` sub-heading under "Getting started" exists but contains zero content. Consumers need guidance on what credentials to supply when using `azd` or the Azure Provisioning infrastructure APIs. - **Empty "Next steps" section**: The `## Next steps` heading exists but has no content — no links to API reference, related provisioning packages (e.g., `Azure.Provisioning.Resources`), or the `azd` deployment workflow. - **`FromExisting` pattern is undocumented**: `ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(...)` allows referencing a pre-existing Resource Graph query in a larger infrastructure definition, which is a key Provisioning library pattern. This method is not mentioned anywhere in the README. - **Output properties not explained**: The read-only output properties `ResultKind`, `ModifiedOn`, and `Id` can be referenced in downstream Bicep expressions. The README has no mention of reading outputs or using them in multi-resource configurations.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` — **edit** 1. Fill in the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section (after the `### Prerequisites` section): ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` is a declarative provisioning library — it generates Bicep/ARM templates rather than calling Azure APIs directly at runtime. Authentication is handled by the deployment tooling: - When using **Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)**, run `azd auth login` to authenticate before deploying. - When deploying programmatically, provide an `ArmClient` with your preferred [`TokenCredential`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) (e.g., `DefaultAzureCredential`). For more information on authentication, see the [Azure Identity README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md). ``` 2. Add a second example under `## Examples` for the `FromExisting` pattern (after the existing query-creation example): ```markdown ### Reference an Existing Resource Graph Query Use `FromExisting` to reference a shared query that is already deployed, so you can wire its outputs into other resources: ```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery)); existingQuery.Name = "my-existing-query"; infra.Add(existingQuery); ``` ``` 3. Fill in the `## Next steps` section: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure Resource Graph documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/governance/resource-graph/overview) - [API reference for Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/azure.provisioning.resourcegraph) - [Azure Provisioning library overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - [Deploy infrastructure with Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/overview) - [Azure.Provisioning.Resources — use ResourceGroup, Subscription, and other ARM primitives alongside your query](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning.Resources/README.md) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **Snippet `ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting`** Write the sample code in a new test file at: `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/Samples/SampleFromExisting.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph.Tests.Samples; public class SampleFromExisting { [Test] public void FromExistingExample() { #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting Infrastructure infra = new(); ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery)); existingQuery.Name = "my-existing-query"; infra.Add(existingQuery); #endregion } } ``` Add the following snippet placeholder in `README.md` under the new "Reference an Existing Resource Graph Query" sub-section (replace the manually-pasted code block shown in Step 1 above): ````markdown ```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting ``` ```` The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the code automatically. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` is currently missing content in these sections: | Section | Status | Action | |---------|--------|--------| | Getting started > Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty | Add content per Step 1 | | Key concepts | ✅ Present (brief, acceptable for initial beta) | No change needed | | Examples | ⚠️ One example only | Add `FromExisting` example per Step 1 | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change needed | | Next steps | ❌ Empty | Add links per Step 1 | | Contributing | ✅ Present | No change needed | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` — confirms README snippet placeholders are filled with code from test files; check `git diff` after running 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` — regenerates public API listing; no new public types expected for this docs-only change 5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [ResourceGraph] Docs: Initial README gaps in Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph 1.0.0-beta.1
